purpose: Define the first measured neural seed experiment without launching training, spending public calibration, or calling the teacher.
objective: Measure whether a small SymLiquid-style proposer improves private residual code proposal quality over a parameter/compute-matched transformer control when both are constrained by the existing verifier, fanout, and STS harness.

allowed_train_sources: private transfer artifacts with no public solutions or tests
allowed_eval_sources: adapter smoke reports with public scoring disabled
forbidden_sources: public benchmark prompts as training rows
teacher-generated rows without explicit operator unlock and retained manifest
data_contract.public_calibration: locked; one-shot only after private gate and operator unlock
data_contract.teacher_distillation: locked; not part of this spec run

matched_budget.parameter_match_rule: Report estimated trainable parameter count for both arms and keep within +/-5 percent before any capability comparison is trusted.
matched_budget.compute_match_rule: Use the same private row count, sequence/token budget, optimizer steps, batch size, seed list, and wall-clock cap for both arms; report any early-stop or backend divergence.

arms.role: candidate proposer only; verifier, fanout, parser masks, and STS context remain external gates
candidate proposer only; same verifier, fanout, parser masks, and STS context

measurement_contract: same private train rows, eval rows, seeds, and STS on/off controls for both arms
same fanout count, parser mask, verifier, ranker, and acceptance policy
report accepted candidate rate, verifier pass rate, syntax pass rate, residual-family movement, regressions, wall-clock, memory, backend, and examples/sec
report STS-on and STS-off rows so STS benefit is visible and not confused with model capacity
no promotion, public calibration, or teacher distillation from this report alone

macos_constraints.apple_silicon: Prefer MLX for tensor/proposer training and report MLX version, unified-memory pressure, thermal/battery state, and fallback reason if CPU is used.
macos_constraints.intel_mac: CPU/storage/operator only for this lane unless a separate non-MLX backend is explicitly implemented and profiled.
macos_constraints.cross_platform: Windows CUDA and Linux CPU/CUDA may run comparable arms later, but this first Mac spec must not assume CUDA on macOS.

neural_student_ready_requires: both arms train under matched budget
both arms evaluate on the same private heldout rows
SymLiquid arm beats matched control on predeclared private metrics without worse regressions
teacher distillation remains locked unless separately operator-unlocked
public calibration remains locked until private gate passes
